members.member: @brief This class is an example of how to load banks in Wwise, if the bank data was preloaded in memory. This would be useful for situations where you use the WWW class
Name of the bank to load
Is the bank localized (situated in the language-specific folders)
Load a SoundBank from WWW object
Load a language-specific bank from WWW object

@brief This manager tracks AkRoomPortals and the rooms that they connect (front and back room). @details At the end of the frame, the AkRoomPortals which rooms might have changed are updated and sent to Spatial Audio.
The reverb auxiliary bus.
Wwise Event to be posted on the room game object.
This is the list of AkRoomAwareObjects that have entered this AkRoom
This is the list of AkRoomAwareObjects that have entered this AkRoom while it was inactive or disabled.
Access the room's ID
Contains all active rooms sorted by priority.
@brief Use this component to add a Large Mode position to any AkAmbient in a Scene. \sa - \ref unity_use_AkEvent_AkAmbient - \ref AkAmbient

member.name: P:AkSpatialAudioListener.SpatialAudioListeners
member.summary: Returns the list of active Unity Game Objects that are designated to be spatial audio listeners.

member.name: T:AkSpatialAudioListener.SpatialAudioListenerList
member.summary: This class represents the list of active Unity Game Objects that are designated to be spatial audio listeners. Currently, only one spatial audio listener can be active at a time.

member.name: M:AkSpatialAudioListener.SpatialAudioListenerList.Add(AkSpatialAudioListener)
member.summary: Uniquely adds listeners to the list

members.member: This allows you to visualize first order reflection sound paths.
This allows you to visualize second order reflection sound paths.
This allows you to visualize third or higher order reflection sound paths.
This allows you to visualize geometric diffraction sound paths between an obstructed emitter and the listener.
Is this object affected by Environment changes? Set to false if not affected in order to save some useless calls. Default is true.
Maintains and persists the Static setting of the gameobject, which is available only in the editor.
Cache the bounds to avoid calls to GetComponent()
When not set to null, the position will be offset relative to the Game Object position by the Position Offset
Gets the position including the position offset, if applyPositionOffset is enabled. User can also override this method to calculate an arbitrary position. \return The position.
Gets the orientation forward vector. User can also override this method to calculate an arbitrary vector. \return The forward vector of orientation.
Gets the orientation upward vector. User can also override this method to calculate an arbitrary vector. \return The upward vector of orientation.
AkRoomPortals can only connect a maximum of 2 rooms.

The front and back rooms connected by the portal. The first room is on the negative side of the portal(opposite to the direction of the local Z axis) The second room is on the positive side of the portal.
The list of rooms sorted by priority in front and in the back of the portal
Access the portal's ID
Opens the portal on trigger event
Closes the portal on trigger event
Delegate declaration for all Wwise Triggers.
All components reacting to the trigger will be registered in this delegate.

@brief This manager tracks the AkRoomAwareObjects and the AkRooms in which they enter and exit. @details At the end of the frame, the AkRoomAwareObject is set in the highest priority AkRoom in Spatial Audio.
@brief Use this component to define an area that straddles two different AkEnvironment's zones and allow mixing between both zones. \ref unity_use_AkEnvironment_AkEnvironmentPortal
The axis used to find the contribution of each environment

The array is already sorted by position. The first environment is on the negative side of the portal(opposite to the direction of the chosen axis) The second environment is on the positive side of the portal
This enables us to detect intersections between portals and environments in the editor
Unity can't serialize an array of list so we wrap the list in a serializable class

member.name: M:AkSurfaceReflector.SetGeometry
member.summary: Sends the mesh's triangles and their acoustic texture to Spatial Audio

member.name: M:AkSurfaceReflector.UpdateGeometry
member.summary: Update the surface reflector's geometry in Spatial Audio.

member.name: M:AkSurfaceReflector.RemoveGeometry
member.summary: Remove the surface reflector's geometry from Spatial Audio.

member.name: T:AkEventCallbackMsg
member.summary: Event callback information. Event callback functions can receive this structure as a parameter

members.member: For more information about the event callback, see the classes derived from AkCallbackInfo.
GameObject from whom the callback function was called
AkSoundEngine.PostEvent callback flags. See the AkCallbackType enumeration for a list of all callbacks
Replacement action. See AK::SoundEngine::ExecuteEventOnAction()
Fade curve to use with the new Action. See AK::SoundEngine::ExecuteEventOnAction()
Enables additional options to reuse existing events. Use it to transform a Play event into a Stop event without having to define one in the Wwise Project.
Game object onto which the Event will be posted. By default, when empty, it is posted on the same object on which the component was added.
Duration of the fade. See AK::SoundEngine::ExecuteEventOnAction()
Decode this SoundBank upon load
Check this to load the SoundBank in the background. Be careful, if Events are triggered and the SoundBank hasn't finished loading, you'll have "Event not found" errors.
Save the decoded SoundBank to disk for faster loads in the future
Reserved.
Loads the SoundBank
Unloads a SoundBank

member.name: M:AkAudioListener.BaseListenerList.Add(AkAudioListener)
member.summary: Uniquely adds listeners to the list

members.member: Since our mask is a 32 bits integer, we can't have more than 32 triggers
Will contain the types of all the triggers derived from AkTriggerBase at runtime
List containing the enabled triggers.
This property is usefull only when used with colliders. When enabled, the target of the action will be the other colliding object. When disabled, it will be the current object.
Contains all active environments sorted by default, excludeOthers and priority, even those inside a portal.
Contains all active environments sorted by priority, even those inside a portal.
Contains all active portals.

member.name: T:AkRoomAwareObject
member.summary: This component makes a GameObject aware of AkRoom components. When using Spatial Audio rooms, all emitters and the spatial audio listener should have this component.

members.member: @brief Sorts AkEnvironments based on their priorities.

@brief Sorts AkEnvironments based on the selection algorithm. The selection algorithm is as follows: -# Environments have priorities. -# Environments have a "Default" flag. This flag effectively says that this environment will be bumped out if any other is present. -# Environments have an "Exclude Other" flag. This flag will tell that this env is not overlappable with others. So, only one (the highest priority) should be selected.

member.name: M:AkSoundEngineController.IsTheSingleOwningInitializer(AkInitializer)
member.summary: Determines whether this AkInitializer is the single one responsible for initializing the sound engine.
member.returns: Returns true when called on the first AkInitializer and false otherwise.
